System Design

Architecture Overview

A fully serverless, event-driven pipeline from API call to email alert — no standing infrastructure required.

Component Breakdown

How It Works

Each AWS service plays a specific role in the detection pipeline.

STEP 01
🔑
IAM API Calls
AWS Identity & Access Management
Users or attackers perform IAM operations. Every API call leaves a footprint that CloudTrail captures in real-time.
ListUsersCreateAccessKeyAttachUserPolicy
STEP 02
📋
CloudTrail Capture
AWS CloudTrail
Captures all management event API activity. Logs are also persisted to S3 for long-term auditing and compliance.
Management EventsS3 Audit Logs
STEP 03
⚡
EventBridge Filtering
Amazon EventBridge
Continuously monitors the CloudTrail event stream. Pattern-matching rules filter noise and route suspicious events in near real-time.
Event Pattern MatchNear Real-Time
STEP 04
λ
Lambda Detection Engine
AWS Lambda (Python)
The intelligence layer. Implements rule-based detection, validates context, and filters false positives before triggering alerts. Zero idle cost.
Rule-Based LogicFalse Positive FilteringContext Validation
STEP 05
🔔
SNS Alert Delivery
Amazon Simple Notification Service
Lambda publishes a structured alert to SNS, which fans out to subscribed email addresses instantly. Extensible to Slack, PagerDuty, or any webhook.
Email AlertsExtensible Fanout
Threat Detection

Detection Capabilities

The Lambda engine identifies two primary attack categories based on IAM API patterns.

LOW → MEDIUM SEVERITY
🔍 Reconnaissance Activity
Attackers enumerate IAM resources to map the environment before escalating. Read-only calls are often the first sign of a breach.
iam:ListUsers
iam:ListRoles
iam:ListPolicies
iam:GetAccountSummary
HIGH SEVERITY
⚠️ Privilege Escalation
Write operations that grant elevated permissions or create new credentials. These indicate active exploitation and require immediate response.
iam:CreateAccessKey
iam:AttachUserPolicy
iam:PutUserPolicy
iam:AddUserToGroup

Architecture Pattern
Project Highlights

Key Features

What makes this project production-relevant and resume-worthy.

⚡
Near Real-Time Detection
EventBridge reacts to CloudTrail events within seconds — no polling, no batch delays.
🔀
Fully Event-Driven
The entire pipeline is trigger-based — resources spin up only when needed, near-zero idle cost.
📡
Automated Alerting
SNS delivers structured email alerts the moment Lambda confirms a threat — no manual intervention.
🧠
False Positive Filtering
Lambda validates event context before alerting — reducing noise and alert fatigue.
🏗️
Serverless Architecture
Zero managed servers. Lambda, EventBridge, and SNS are fully managed — pay per invocation only.
🔬
AWS Native Tooling
No third-party agents or SIEMs — built entirely with AWS-native services for tighter integration.
Honest Assessment

Limitations & Roadmap

No project is perfect — here's what the current version doesn't cover, and where it's headed next.

⚠️ Current Limitations

◆
Depends on CloudTrail being active. If disabled or tampered with, detection fails entirely.
◆
Only monitors management events — data events (S3 GetObject, DynamoDB reads) are not yet in scope.
◆
Rule-based detection can generate false positives in environments with automated IAM workflows or CI/CD pipelines.
◆
Single-account scope — no multi-account or AWS Organizations aggregation yet.

🚀 Future Improvements

◆
Add S3 data event monitoring to catch data exfiltration attempts (GetObject at scale).
◆
Implement Lambda-based auto-remediation — automatically revoke suspicious access keys on detection.
◆
Multi-account centralized logging using AWS Organizations and a dedicated Security account.
◆
Terraform-based IaC to provision the entire pipeline — reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure.
